appeal_letter:
Re: Appeal of Denial — Semaglutide (Wegovy)

Dear Cigna Appeals Reviewer,

I am writing to appeal the denial of the request for Semaglutide (Wegovy). This letter asks that the decision be reconsidered based on the specific clinical purpose for which the medication was prescribed.

The denial gave two grounds. First, it stated that the employer-sponsored pharmacy benefit plan has a strict exclusion for all weight-loss medications. Second, it stated that the member does not have a diagnosis of Type 2 Diabetes, which would be required for coverage of similar GLP-1 medications under the medical benefit. On those grounds the request was denied as a plan exclusion and as not medically necessary.

I want to address the strongest of these grounds — the plan exclusion — directly rather than around it. The exclusion the denial cites is for weight-loss medications. The treating clinician's documented purpose for this prescription is not weight loss as an end in itself. The clinical context indicates the member is on the verge of developing Type 2 Diabetes and that the clinician prescribed the medication to prevent the onset of diabetes. The relevant question for this appeal is therefore whether a medication prescribed to prevent the onset of a serious metabolic disease falls within an exclusion written for weight-loss drugs. I ask the reviewer to evaluate the prescription by its documented clinical indication — diabetes prevention in a high-risk patient — rather than solely by the drug's general category.

If the plan maintains that the exclusion applies on its face regardless of indication, I ask that the appeal be evaluated for a medical-necessity exception based on the individualized facts. The denial's second ground notes that a Type 2 Diabetes diagnosis would support coverage of similar GLP-1 medications under the medical benefit. The clinical context is that the member is at the threshold of that very diagnosis. I ask the reviewer to consider whether it is consistent with the plan's own stated criteria to require the member to progress to established Type 2 Diabetes before a preventive intervention the clinician has identified can be covered, when timely treatment may prevent that diagnosis.

I also note a procedural point. The denial as provided does not state the deadline for filing this appeal, the process for submitting it, or the member's right to a full and fair review. I respectfully assert the member's right to a complete and fair review and request that the applicable appeal rights, deadlines, and procedures be provided in writing.

Requested action: I ask that Cigna overturn the denial and approve Semaglutide (Wegovy) on the basis of its documented purpose of preventing the onset of Type 2 Diabetes; or alternatively provide a written explanation of how the weight-loss-medication exclusion applies to a prescription written specifically for diabetes prevention, together with any medical-necessity exception process available to the member.

The prescribing clinician can provide supporting documentation, including the clinical rationale and relevant laboratory results, on request.

Not legal or medical advice. Draft assistance only.

Respectfully,
Member
missing_evidence_checklist: Prescribing clinician's statement documenting that Wegovy was prescribed to prevent the onset of Type 2 Diabetes rather than for weight loss
Laboratory results and clinical findings establishing the member's high risk of developing Type 2 Diabetes (for example, glycemic markers)
The exact plan language of the weight-loss-medication exclusion and any indication-based or medical-necessity exceptions
The coverage criteria under the medical benefit for GLP-1 medications, including any prevention or pre-diabetes provisions
Documentation of any prior interventions tried and their outcomes
The applicable appeal deadline, filing procedure, and statement of the member's review rights
